instruction:
Complete a verifiable landscape photo enhancement workflow using the image at /home/user/Desktop/camera-man-donkey-5431685_1920.jpg. Each step produces measurable, verifiable outputs:

1. Create a new layer named exactly 'Sky_Enhance' above the background. Use the Free Select tool to select the sky area (top 35% of the image). Apply Hue-Saturation adjustment: increase Blue saturation by exactly +35 and Lightness by exactly -15 to make the sky more vibrant and dramatic.

2. Create a new layer named exactly 'Mountain_Contrast' above 'Sky_Enhance'. Use the Curves adjustment to create an S-curve: darken shadows by 20% and brighten highlights by 15% to enhance the mountain details.

3. Create a new layer named exactly 'Donkey_Warmth' above 'Mountain_Contrast'. Use the Color Balance adjustment on the donkey and foreground grass area: add +20 to Red channel and +15 to Yellow channel to create a warm golden hour effect.

4. Create a new layer named exactly 'Vignette' at the top of the layer stack. Use the Elliptical Select tool to create an oval covering 75% of the image from center, apply Select > Feather with 80px radius, invert selection, and fill with black at 25% opacity.

5. Add a text layer named exactly 'Text_Title' at the top-left corner with these exact specifications:
   - Content: 'ALPINE DREAM'
   - Font size: 36px
   - Color: #ffffff (white)
   - Position: 30px from left edge, 30px from top edge
   - Layer name: exactly 'Text_Title'

6. Add a text layer named exactly 'Text_Location' at the bottom-right corner with these exact specifications:
   - Content: 'SWISS ALPS'
   - Font size: 24px
   - Color: #ffd700 (gold)
   - Position: 30px from right edge, 30px from bottom edge
   - Layer name: exactly 'Text_Location'

7. Create a new layer named exactly 'Frame' at the very top. Use Select > All, then Edit > Stroke Selection with a 10px solid color #2f4f4f (dark slate gray) to create a border around the entire image.

8. Export the final image as 'alpine_masterpiece.jpg' with exact specifications: JPEG format, width 1600 pixels (height auto), quality 95. Save the project as 'alpine_project.xcf' with all layers preserved.

command: import pyautogui; import time; pyautogui.hotkey('shift', 'ctrl', 'e'); time.sleep(1); pyautogui.write('alpine_masterpiece'); time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.press('enter'); time.sleep(2); pyautogui.press('enter')

import pyautogui; import time; pyautogui.hotkey('shift', 'ctrl', 's'); time.sleep(1); pyautogui.write('alpine_project.xcf'); time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.press('enter'); time.sleep(2); pyautogui.press('enter')
